codeReview.summary: The feature cleanly adds account-scoped multi-device registration and a device listing endpoint, with strong unit/integration/CLI coverage for distinct device identity under one account and restart determinism. I found two non-blocking gaps: SSE-authenticated requests bypass the auto-registration path, and the dedicated new tests do not directly assert VAL-CROSS-006 secure message continuity (key exchange + decrypt) for same-account multi-device flows.

issues.description: `/events` returns before the auto-registration block (`accountStore.registerDevice(...)` at line 458), so SSE-only authenticated devices are not registered even though comments/handoff describe auto-registration on every authenticated request.

The new feature tests validate account/device identity and session determinism, but they do not directly assert multi-device key-exchange/decrypt continuity; VAL-CROSS-006 evidence in the contract requires authenticated multi-device secure-message continuity with decrypt results.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill scope text appears stale relative to current mission usage: this feature uses `mors-global-worker` in `onboarding-handle-profile`, but the skill's 'When to Use' section lists only older milestones.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/mors-global-worker/SKILL.md:12 lists `relay-foundation`, `global-async-messaging`, `realtime-watch`, `developer-launch-path`; mission features assign `mors-global-worker` to onboarding-handle-profile entries (features.json:1578-1580).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Procedure/manual-check expectations are inconsistent with this run's reporting: the skill requires manual CLI/API/SSE sanity checks (or explicit rationale if skipped), while this handoff reports only automated commands and still marks followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/mors-global-worker/SKILL.md:31-33 requires manual checks or explicit skip rationale; handoff has `verification.commandsRun` and `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true` without an `interactiveChecks` section (/Users/justen/.factory/missions/54bd84c5-2680-4f00-890c-9c832860b3d4/handoffs/2026-02-28T23-09-27-565Z__native-multi-device-bootstrap-continuity__f8537cbe-c714-49cd-afd4-df0a71c72e2d.json:13-15,117-119).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission convention guidance and test-code practice diverge on shell invocation patterns for dynamic args.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS forbids dynamic shell command strings and recommends argv-based subprocess calls (AGENTS.md:42), but this feature's new test uses interpolated `execSync` command strings (test/auth/multi-device-bootstrap.test.ts:462).

summary: Pass with non-blocking findings: implementation quality is strong for account/device identity continuity and deterministic session behavior, but `/events` bypasses the new auto-registration path and dedicated VAL-CROSS-006 secure-delivery continuity coverage is indirect rather than explicit in this feature's new tests.
